member.name: T:Deveel.Filters.FilterExpressionParser
member.summary: Provides functionality to parse string expressions into Filter objects using DynamicExpressionParser.

member.name: M:Deveel.Filters.FilterExpressionParser.Parse``1(System.String,System.String,System.Linq.Dynamic.Core.ParsingConfig)
member.summary: Parses a string expression into a Filter object with a typed parameter.
member.typeparam: The type of the parameter in the expression
member.returns: A Filter object representing the parsed expression

member.name: T:Deveel.Filters.ExpressionToFilterVisitor
member.summary: Visitor class that navigates Expression trees and converts them to Filter objects.

member.name: M:Deveel.Filters.ExpressionToFilterVisitor.ConvertToFilter(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression)
member.summary: Converts an Expression to a Filter object.

member.name: T:Deveel.Filters.FilterExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for converting Filter objects into dynamic LINQ expressions.

member.name: M:Deveel.Filters.FilterExtensions.AsDynamicLambda(Deveel.Filters.Filter,System.Type,System.String,System.Linq.Dynamic.Core.ParsingConfig)
member.summary: Converts a Filter object into a LambdaExpression that can be used with DynamicLinq for filtering.
member.param: The filter to convert into a LambdaExpression. This filter will be converted into a string representation that is compatible with DynamicLinq parsing, and then parsed back into a LambdaExpression.
The type of the parameter that will be used in the LambdaExpression. This is typically the type of the entity being filtered. The parameter name in the expression will be determined by the argument.

The name of the parameter to be used in the LambdaExpression. This is the name that will be used in the string representation of the filter when it is converted for DynamicLinq parsing. If this parameter is null or whitespace, the parameter will be defined without a name in the LambdaExpression, and the string representation of the filter will not include a parameter name. This can be useful in cases where the parameter name is not relevant or should be inferred from context.
member.returns: Returns a that represents the filter, which can be used to filter an using DynamicLinq. The resulting LambdaExpression will have a single parameter of the specified type, and its body will represent the logic defined in the original Filter object.
member.exception: Thrown if either or is null.

Thrown if the filter cannot be converted into a valid LambdaExpression, which can occur if the filter contains unsupported operations or if the resulting string representation is not compatible with DynamicLinq parsing.

member.name: M:Deveel.Filters.FilterExtensions.ToDynamicString(Deveel.Filters.Filter)
member.summary: Converts a Filter object into a string that is compatible with DynamicLinq parsing.
member.param: The filter to convert to a DynamicLinq-compatible string.
member.returns: A string representation of the filter that can be parsed by DynamicLinq.

member.name: T:Deveel.Filters.DynamicLinqFilterStringBuilder
member.summary: A specialized filter visitor that formats filters in a way compatible with DynamicLinq parsing.
